---
title: "Full extdata gallery"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    fig_width: 7
    fig_height: 4.5
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Full extdata gallery}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
library(ggPopi)

ggpop_extdata <- function(...) {
  system.file("extdata", ..., package = "ggPopi", mustWork = TRUE)
}
```

This gallery uses the full example data shipped in `inst/extdata`.
It is the closest match to the package's real-world supported examples.

## GWAS

```{r, fig.alt = c("Manhattan plot from the bundled GCTA result file. Chromosomes are arranged along the x-axis and minus log10 p-values are on the y-axis, with horizontal reference lines marking GWAS thresholds.", "Q-Q scatter plot from the bundled GCTA result file. Expected minus log10 p-values are on the x-axis and observed minus log10 p-values are on the y-axis, with points compared against a diagonal reference line.")}
gwas <- import_gwas(ggpop_extdata("gwas", "gcta.mlma"), type = "gcta")
plot_manha(gwas)
plot_qq(gwas)
```

## PCA

```{r, fig.alt = "Scatter chart from the bundled GCTA PCA eigenvector file. Principal components define the axes and point colour encodes imported population groups from the shared pop_group.txt metadata."}
pca <- import_pca(
  ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenvec"),
  type = "gcta",
  eigenval = ggpop_extdata("pca", "gcta.eigenval"),
  pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt")
)
plot_pca(pca)
```

## Admixture

```{r, fig.alt = "Faceted stacked bar chart from the full bundled ADMIXTURE results. Each K panel shows individuals as bars, ancestry proportions as stacked segments, and population labels used to group samples."}
admix <- import_admix(
  ggpop_extdata("admixture"),
  type = "admixture",
  ind = ggpop_extdata("snp", "finalsnp_ld.fam"),
  pop_group = ggpop_extdata("pop_group.txt")
)
plot_admix(admix, k = "all", order_group = TRUE)
```

The same data also works with the layered path:

```{r, fig.alt = "Stacked bar chart for K equals 3 from the layered ggplot workflow. Individuals are grouped by population metadata and each bar shows ancestry proportions summing to one."}
ggpop(admix) + geom_admix(k = 3, order_group = TRUE)
```
